28.reason: [Error: malloc returns void*, needs explicit cast in C++ but not in C; however, the allocated size is too small (100 bytes) for the string s which can be up to 10086 characters, leading to buffer overflow]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: malloc returns void*, needs explicit cast in C++ but not in C; however, the allocated size is too small (100 bytes) for the string s which can be up to 10086 characters, leading to buffer overflow]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: malloc returns void*, but q is char*; also, the size calculation is incorrect and may cause issues; more importantly, q is later assigned to s, causing a memory leak]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Allocated memory is never freed and pointer is overwritten; leads to memory leak.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  allocated memory is never used and causes a memory leak

71.reason: [Error: This loop increments q by strlen(m)+1, which skips the entire word plus one character, but the intention is to move to the next word; it should skip only the current word and the null terminator, but the logic is flawed and may cause out-of-bounds access]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: This loop increments q by strlen(m)+1, which skips the entire word plus one character, but the intention is to move to the next word; it should skip only the current word and the null terminator, but the logic is flawed and may cause out-of-bounds access]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: This loop incorrectly advances q by the length of the string plus one, but q points to the start of the current word; it should skip to the next word after the null terminator, but the logic is flawed and may cause out-of-bounds access]
